summary: Russian forces reported liberating 172 buildings in Konstantinovka and killing up to 40 Ukrainian soldiers, while Ukrainian units destroyed six Russian UAV launch points in Pokrovsk. Ukraine struck a Russian oil‑processing facility in Kotovo, Volgograd region, causing a fire, and Russian KAB attacks hit civilian infrastructure in Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk region, injuring nine. Both sides continue intensive UAV activity across multiple fronts, with numerous sightings and interceptions reported.
key_points: Russia claims to have liberated 172 buildings in Konstantinovka and eliminated up to 40 Ukrainian soldiers in a 24‑hour period (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Ukrainian forces reported destroying six Russian UAV launch points in Pokrovsk (Source: @UkraineNow/81893).
A Russian oil‑processing plant near Kotovo in Volgograd region was hit, fire confirmed (Source: @UkraineNow/81894).
Russian KAB attacks in Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk region injured nine civilians, damaged a market, shops and a multi‑apartment building (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Russian air defence shot down 15 guided bombs and 540 UAVs of Ukrainian origin in one day (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Ukrainian drones attacked infrastructure in occupied Crimea and Krasnodar, Russian authorities say 177 drones were intercepted and fires sparked at Temryuk terminal (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
Russian pilots used FAB‑3000 bombs to destroy temporary Ukrainian dislocation points in the Donetsk People’s Republic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30867).
Multiple UAV sightings reported in Chernihiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Poltava and other regions, indicating ongoing low‑altitude activity (Source: @eRadarrua/96351).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces announced the liberation of 172 buildings in Konstantinovka and the loss of up to 40 Ukrainian soldiers, while Ukrainian troops eliminated six Russian UAV launch sites in Pokrovsk. Ukraine struck a Russian oil‑processing facility in Kotovo, Volgograd region, igniting a fire, and Russian KAB attacks in Vasylkivka caused civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Both sides continue intense UAV operations across several fronts, with numerous interceptions and sightings reported.

points: Ukrainian units destroyed six Russian UAV launch points in Pokrovsk, targeting sites near a café, a children’s goods store and other civilian buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81893).
Ukrainian forces reported a Leopard‑2 tank loss in the combat zone, according to Russian defence ministry data (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Ukrainian drones attacked infrastructure in occupied Crimea and Krasnodar, with Russian officials claiming 177 drones intercepted and fires at the Temryuk sea terminal (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
Ukrainian UAV activity was observed over Chernihiv (multiple points moving south‑west), Dnipropetrovsk (near Kamianke and Apostolove) and Poltava (towards V. Baghachka) (Source: @eRadarrua/96351).
Ukrainian pilots reportedly used FAB‑3000 bombs to strike temporary dislocation points in the Donetsk People’s Republic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30867).
Russian forces claimed liberation of 172 buildings in Konstantinovka and the elimination of up to 40 Ukrainian soldiers within 24 hours (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Russian air defence reported shooting down 15 guided aerial bombs and 540 Ukrainian UAVs of airplane type in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/380480).

Russian KAB attacks in Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk region injured nine civilians, set fire to a local market and damaged eight shops, ten stalls and a multi‑storey residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Russian authorities reported the destruction of a Ukrainian temporary dislocation point using FAB‑3000 bombs in the Donetsk People’s Republic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30867).
Russian officials announced the creation of at least five new launch sites for strike drones near the Belarusian border (Source: @UkraineNow/81886).
Ukrainian UAV sightings reported over Chernihiv region moving south‑west toward Kyiv, and a group heading east along the border with Belarus toward Kyiv (Source: @kpszsu/64972).
Russian claims of heavy casualties among Ukrainian troops in the eastern zone, with approximately 1,440 Ukrainian soldiers lost across all groupings (Source: @tass_agency/380480).

Ukrainian drones attacked infrastructure in occupied Crimea and Krasnodar, with fires reported at the Temryuk terminal and damage to the Chongar bridge linking Crimea and occupied Kherson (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
Ukrainian UAV activity observed near Kamianke in Dnipropetrovsk region, indicating pressure on supply routes (Source: @eRadarrua/96351).
Russian air defence intercepted a large number of Ukrainian UAVs, suggesting intensified aerial contest over the eastern front (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Ukrainian UAVs reported over Chernihiv region along the Dnipro River heading toward Dobryanka (Source: @kpszsu/65015).
Russian pilots used FAB‑3000 bombs against Ukrainian temporary points in the Donetsk People’s Republic, affecting northern sector operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30867).
Ukrainian UAV sightings in Chernihiv include four units over Lyubech/Slavutych and one over Snovsk (Source: @eRadarrua/96351).
Russian air defence claimed downing 540 UAVs of airplane type, many likely operating from the north (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Ukrainian UAVs observed moving from Chernihiv toward Kyiv direction, potentially targeting logistics hubs (Source: @kpszsu/65010).
Russian KAB attacks in Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk region caused civilian injuries and damage to market infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Ukrainian UAV sightings reported over Mykolaiv region heading north, and over Dnipropetrovsk toward Marhanets (Source: @kpszsu/65017).
Ukrainian drones attacked a critical infrastructure object in Zaporizhzhia, resulting in one fatality (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Ukrainian UAV activity noted over the Black Sea heading toward Chornomorsk and further toward Tatarbunary (Source: @eRadarrua/96356).
Russian air defence reported intercepting 110 of 118 strike drones targeting southern regions (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Russian air defence shot down 15 guided aerial bombs and 540 Ukrainian UAVs of airplane type in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Ukrainian drones conducted attacks on occupied Crimea and Krasnodar, with Russian reports of 177 drones intercepted and fires at the Temryuk sea terminal (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
Ukrainian UAV sightings across multiple regions (Chernihiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Poltava) indicate sustained low‑altitude operations (Source: @eRadarrua/96351).
Russian KAB launches in Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk region resulted in civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Ukrainian forces reported destroying six Russian UAV launch points in Pokrovsk, reducing enemy strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81893).

key_developments: Russian forces claim liberation of 172 buildings in Konstantinovka and the killing of up to 40 Ukrainian soldiers, indicating a localized offensive gain (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Ukrainian troops destroyed six Russian UAV launch sites in Pokrovsk, degrading enemy aerial strike capacity (Source: @UkraineNow/81893).
A fire erupted at a Russian oil‑processing facility near Kotovo, Volgograd region after Ukrainian strike, affecting fuel logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81894).
Russian KAB attacks in Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk region injured nine civilians and damaged market infrastructure, showing continued targeting of civilian areas (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Russian air defence reported downing 15 guided bombs and 540 UAVs, reflecting intense aerial contest (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Ukrainian drones attacked infrastructure in occupied Crimea and Krasnodar; Russian authorities say 177 drones were intercepted and fires started at Temryuk terminal (Source: @eRadarrua/96377).
Russian pilots employed FAB‑3000 bombs to destroy Ukrainian temporary dislocation points in the Donetsk People’s Republic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30867).
sections.threat_assessment: Both sides are intensifying UAV operations, with Russia deploying large numbers of KABs and claiming high interception rates, while Ukraine continues to target logistical nodes, including oil processing facilities and enemy UAV launch sites. The reported loss of a Leopard‑2 tank and significant Ukrainian casualties suggest sustained attrition on the ground. Russian air defence successes may limit Ukrainian strike depth, but continued attacks on civilian infrastructure raise the risk of broader destabilisation.
sections.regional_response: Local authorities in the affected Dnipropetrovsk districts have reported civilian injuries and property damage, prompting emergency response measures. Russian statements about new drone launch sites near the Belarusian border indicate a strategic shift to increase strike capacity from the north. Ukrainian leadership has called for heightened public vigilance regarding air alerts, reflecting concern over the expanding aerial threat.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows a pattern of south‑west movement from Chernihiv toward Kyiv and eastward trajectories from Dnipropetrovsk, suggesting coordinated targeting of supply corridors. The use of FAB‑3000 bombs by Russian pilots marks an escalation in munition types against Ukrainian command and control nodes. Interception data points to a high volume of Ukrainian aerial assets, necessitating continued SIGINT focus on drone launch and control sites.
